rulings.Case Title: EICON INC VS WORLDPOINT INTERACTIVE INC

DEFENDANT SNYDER TRUST ENTERPRISES, JOHN POPPIN, 706 SANSOME PROPERTIES, MARGARET POPPIN'S Motion To Require Cross-Defendant Massimo Fuchs To Be Declared A Vexatious Litigant (CCP 391 Et Seq.) is off calendar. Matter set to be heard in Department 206. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EDNA CANNON et al VS. CRYSTAL IDELLA CANNON et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 13, 2010, line 2. CROSS COMPLAINANT MAHER MUHAWIEH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Motion denied as to 1st cause of action (Elder Abuse). Triable issue of fact raised whether there was a fraud, and whether defendant knew or should have known of the wrongdoing. (Tigner deposition). Motion denied as to 8th cause of action (declaratory relief). Triable issue of fact as to who remains the owner of the property. Motion granted as to remaining causes of action. Plaintiff does not address the 2nd, 4th, 5th and 6th causes of action in his opposition and no triable issue of fact raised. The 3rd cause of action fails because no fiduciary or confidential relationship essential to a claim for constructive fraud is established. The 7th cause of action for negligence fails because the buyer owed the sellers no duty. The knowledge of a dual agent does not impute to the principal. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS YEP VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 13, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, RAFAEL RODRIGUEZ, OMAR CARVALLO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The Court has yet to determine the 1st and 2nd causes of action. The court did not execute an order regarding the motion for judgment on the pleadings and the time to amend has not yet run. Motion for summary adjudication granted as to the F E H A retaliation, harassment, and discrimination causes of action. First, nearly all of plaintiff's claims are time-barred and the continuing violation doctrine does not apply. Second, plaintiff's complaint regarding the civil service commission's conduct lacks merit. Plaintiff fails to show pretext. Third, plaintiff fails to show harassing conduct or that he exhausted his administrative remedy. Fourth, individual defendants cannot be liable for retaliation. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ABE NEWMAN et al VS. SURESH PARMAR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 13, 2010, line 10. DEFENDANT SURESH PARMAR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled, 5 days leave to answer. The F D C P A applies to household consumer debts and this case involves a commercial lease. Furthermore, even if the landlord failed to comply with the F D C P A, that may be grounds for an affirmative action under that statute but it is not a defense to unlawful detainer. See Missionary Sisters of the Sacred Heart, Inc. v. Robert Dowling (1999) 703 N.Y.S. 2d 362, 367. =(302/CWW)
